data.title: Where EV Stocks Are Going In 2025
data.description: In Oct, new EV sales up 2.3% and surpassed 1M units for the yr. But EV stocks aren't performing well. Challenges include consumer confusion, affordability, and Trump's EV policies. Tesla stands out with 34% stock increase. Chargepoint also a potential investment in the EV market.

data.title: Nike's Earnings Loom With Bearish Signals: Will The Swoosh Bounce Back?
data.description: Nike (NKE) to report Q2 earnings on Thursday. Wall Street expects 64 cents EPS and $12.13 billion revenues. Stock down 37.30% over past year, but analysts see 17% upside potential.

data.title: Your Healthcare Costs are Soaring! US Spending Reached $5 Trillion In 2023 with Major Increase in Obesity and Diabetes Drugs, Hospital Services
data.description: U.S. healthcare spending grew 7.5% to $4.9 trillion in 2023, with hospital care and prescription drugs driving costs. Private insurance accounted for 30% of total spending.

data.title: Merck's Investigational Two-Drug Regime For HIV Meets Primary Goal In Two Late-Stage Trials
data.description: Merck shares Phase 3 trial results for its HIV-1 regimen, doravirine/islatravir, showing safety and comparable efficacy to existing therapies.

data.title: Spotlight on Chevron: Analyzing the Surge in Options Activity
